text.title: Accessibility Worked Example
text.summary: One worked end-to-end example showing a Accessibility artefact filled from a realistic input bundle. Trace runs from input brief → evidence gathering → schema fill → validator pass → review surfacing.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Solo founder publishes a B2B dashboard targeting EU customers. Spec: WCAG-2.1-AA, tools=[axe-core, axe-playwright, NVDA, VoiceOver], CI gate=axe-playwright on 4 critical routes, responsibilities mapped per WCAG category, third-party Intercom chat policy='escalate within 30 days or wrap', regression test policy='every a11y fix ships a Playwright assertion'.

list.item: Step 1 — prerequisites_ok=true; all input refs reachable.
Step 2 — evidence array populated with ≥1 citation per non-trivial field.
Step 4 — validator script exits 0; no violations.

section.title: Outcome
section.p: The artefact is auditable by a downstream reviewer without re-deriving the reasoning. Every non-trivial field traces back to an evidence row; rejected fields surface as structured skip-with-reason instead of polluting the library with degraded output.
